comment: Default-FAIL verification contract. Every check starts at passes=false. A check may be flipped to true only after its verification command ran and the evidence file (log, report, or screenshot path relative to the repository root) was inspected. check-contract.(sh|ps1) refuses Done while any required check fails. Checks with required:false that have passes=false must carry a non-empty waiver_reason explaining why the check does not apply.

checks.waiver_reason: Non-code stack (stack: docs/shell per Global Constraints): this task edits Markdown, JSON marketplace data, and a PowerShell test-expectation script; there is no linter configured for this repo's Markdown/PowerShell/JSON files, so lint is not applicable per risk-gate-matrix.md's stack descriptor.
Non-code stack: no compiled-language toolchain applies to Markdown/PowerShell/JSON files edited by this task.

The real, unmodified tests/validate-repository.ps1 cannot exit 0 on this host: it fails at a pre-existing, out-of-scope precondition in plugins/sdd-quality-loop/scripts/check-workflow-state.ps1, whose Test-Json call requires PowerShell 6.1+ and is absent on this host's Windows PowerShell 5.1.22621.5697 (independently confirmed: Get-Command Test-Json returns nothing; git-stash of all uncommitted sdd-domain work reproduces the identical failure). This is unrelated to T-010's own edits. passes=true is based instead on independent direct-count verification of this task's real acceptance criteria: 7 plugin manifest directories (find plugins -path '*/.claude-plugin/plugin.json' | wc -l = 7) exactly matching $expectedPlugins.Count in the script; 26 SKILL.md files under plugins/ exactly matching $expectedSkills (set-diff against the script's literal array is empty); exactly 6 skills lacking 'user-invocable: false' (bootstrap, domain-model, diagnose, fix-by-review-ticket, sdd-sudo, ship) exactly matching $userVisibleSkills; and all three sdd-domain plugin.json manifests (.claude-plugin, .codex-plugin, .plugin) plus both marketplace.json files version-locked to 1.8.0 matching $expectedVersions. See T-010.unit-tests.log for the real full-script failure output (documenting the pre-existing blocker) and this waiver_reason for the direct-count evidence that substitutes for it.
No integration surface: this task only edits test-expectation constants and documentation/data files, not integrated runtime components.
Non-code stack: Markdown/PowerShell/JSON files have no build step.
No startable dev server, Docker container, or CLI binary: this task's deliverables are a test-expectation script and Markdown/JSON documentation, not a running service.
Not applicable: no behavioral baseline to diff against for documentation/test-expectation updates.
No UI surface is touched by this task.
No design-system artifact is touched by this task.

Done-When requires validate-repository.ps1 to pass cleanly (7 plugins, 26 skills, 6 public skills, all manifests version-locked). The full script cannot run to completion on this host due to the pre-existing, out-of-scope Test-Json/PowerShell-5.1 blocker in check-workflow-state.ps1 (see unit-tests check for full detail and reproduction). Acceptance evidence is therefore the independent direct-count verification performed for this review: 7 plugin manifest directories found (matches $expectedPlugins.Count=7); 26 SKILL.md files found, set-identical to the script's $expectedSkills array; exactly 6 skills lack 'user-invocable: false' (bootstrap, domain-model, diagnose, fix-by-review-ticket, sdd-sudo, ship), set-identical to $userVisibleSkills; sdd-domain version-locked to 1.8.0 across all three plugin.json manifests and both marketplace.json files, matching $expectedVersions. This substitutes for a full clean-script-exit because the blocker preventing that exit is confirmed pre-existing and unrelated to this task.
